codeReview.summary: Atomic temp-file+fsync+rename writes for session state and .flow/active are implemented and targeted tests are added, but the in-process lock does not actually serialize full saveSession calls as required.

issues.description: The mutex is scoped to saveSessionState (lines 40-49), but saveSession immediately performs syncSessionArtifacts outside that lock (line 64). Because syncSessionArtifacts calls renderSessionDocs (src/runtime/render.ts lines 38-63, multiple writeFile/rm calls), two concurrent saveSession calls can interleave artifact writes and let an older save overwrite docs from a newer save. This violates the requirement to serialize concurrent saveSession calls on the same worktree.

withSessionSaveLock stores previous.then(() => current) in sessionSaveQueues (lines 81-84) but compares the map value to current for cleanup (line 92). That equality is never true because then() returns a distinct promise, so per-worktree queue entries are never deleted after completion (memory leak over long-lived processes/worktrees).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The core-worker procedure does not document that formatter write commands may be blocked in worker mode, which caused explicit adaptation during this run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback at /Users/vriesd/.factory/missions/34ec2e58-6360-41eb-aaf2-3aba539ddf02/handoffs/2026-04-18T13-05-22-194Z__m2-atomic-writes__436f31e6-21e9-4566-a0cd-edef0084df79.json notes: "The core-worker skill should mention that formatter write commands can be blocked in worker mode, so manual formatting may be necessary."

summary: Failing review: atomic write mechanics are present, but the lock does not cover the full saveSession operation, leaving concurrent doc artifact races possible and violating the feature’s serialization requirement.
